summary: Completed testing of Google Play Billing integration and updated subscription prices. All backend APIs (10/10 tests passed) and frontend features working correctly. Prices updated to $4.99/$9.99/$19.99. Google Play Billing endpoints functional (MOCKED server-side verification).
critical_code_review_comments: Google Play server-side verification is MOCKED - currently trusts purchase tokens from client without calling Google Play Developer API. Full verification requires Google Cloud Service Account JSON.

test_credentials.note: User was upgraded to Pro tier via Google Play verify test call

seed_data_creation: Test user subscription upgraded to Pro tier during Google Play verify endpoint test
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration 5 features tested and working. Google Play Billing integration is functional but server-side verification is MOCKED (trusts client tokens). Test user testplumber@test.com is now on Pro tier (active subscription via Google Play verify test).
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials returns token
Backend: GET /api/subscriptions/google-play/products returns 3 products with correct prices and product IDs
Backend: POST /api/subscriptions/google-play/verify works with test purchase token
Backend: POST /api/subscriptions/google-play/verify rejects invalid product ID
Frontend: Login page accepts credentials and navigates to dashboard
Frontend: Dashboard loads with 13 quick access cards
Frontend: Subscription page shows updated prices: Basic $4.99, Pro $9.99, Enterprise $19.99
Frontend: Subscription page shows 3 tier cards with correct data-testid attributes
Frontend: Subscription page shows 'You're subscribed to PRO' banner
Frontend: Plumbing Code page displays 11 chapters with quick jump chips

google_play_billing.products_endpoint: Returns 3 products with correct prices and product IDs
google_play_billing.verify_endpoint: Accepts purchase tokens and updates user subscription

mocked_apis.google_play_server_verification: Server-side Google Play Developer API verification is stubbed. Currently trusts purchase tokens from client and records them without calling Google API. Full verification requires Google Cloud Service Account JSON.
